assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with MIXED — both additive and reductive signals are present across the test file changes
assertion_results.evidence: The report.md summary table row reads: '| Test Change Classification | MIXED | Modified test file has both reductive signals (removed test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers, relaxed assertion in test_recommend_purls_basic) and additive signals (added test_recommend_purls_dedup); new test file tests/api/purl_simplify.rs is purely additive |'. The classification is explicitly MIXED with both additive and reductive signals identified.

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings identify the removed test function test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers as a reductive signal
assertion_results.evidence: The report.md reductive findings section states: 'tests/api/purl_recommend.rs: test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers removed -- this function verified that PURLs with different qualifiers for the same version were returned as separate entries.' The structural summary also lists '-1 test function (test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers)'.

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings note the assertion change in test_recommend_purls_basic — the PURL assertion changed from checking a fully qualified PURL with qualifiers to checking a versioned PURL without qualifiers, which the semantic assessment may identify as a relaxation contributing to the MIXED classification
assertion_results.evidence: The report.md structural summary states: '1 assertion relaxed: assert_eq!(body.items[0].purl, "pkg:maven/...@3.12?repository_url=...&type=jar") replaced with assert_eq!(body.items[0].purl, "pkg:maven/...@3.12") -- the old assertion verified the complete PURL including qualifiers, the new one checks only the versioned PURL'. The reductive findings also state: 'assertion in test_recommend_purls_basic relaxed -- old assertion checked the complete PURL string including qualifiers; new assertion checks only the versioned PURL.' The semantic assessment notes this as a reductive signal contributing to the MIXED classification.

assertion_results.text: The new test file tests/api/purl_simplify.rs is recognized as additive (3 new test functions)
assertion_results.evidence: The report.md structural summary states: 'tests/api/purl_simplify.rs (new): +3 test functions, +10 assertions (purely additive, no base-branch comparison needed)'. The summary table also mentions 'new test file tests/api/purl_simplify.rs is purely additive'.

assertion_results.text: The new test function test_recommend_purls_dedup in the modified file is recognized as an additive signal
assertion_results.evidence: The report.md summary table states 'additive signals (added test_recommend_purls_dedup)' for the modified test file. The structural summary lists '+1 test function (test_recommend_purls_dedup)' under tests/api/purl_recommend.rs.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only report files: report.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md. These are all analysis/reporting documents. The report describes reading and analyzing PR diff content, CI status, and test files but does not indicate any code modifications were made. No code files appear in the outputs.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: The report ends with findings presented for review. The Test Change Classification section concludes with 'the structural removal of a test function and relaxation of an assertion are reductive signals that warrant human review to confirm the coverage trade-off is acceptable.' There is no mention of merging the PR anywhere in the outputs. The report footer states 'This comment was AI-generated by sdlc-workflow/verify-pr'.

assertion_results.text: The test change classification verdict appears in the verification report summary table and is accompanied by a detailed analysis section explaining the structural and semantic assessment
assertion_results.evidence: The summary table contains row '| Test Change Classification | MIXED | ...' (line 14 of report.md). The detailed analysis section titled '#### Test Change Classification -- MIXED' (line 165) contains subsections: 'Structural summary' listing function/assertion additions, removals, and relaxations for each file; 'Semantic assessment' explaining why the signals are intentional; and 'Reductive findings' with specific details about removed/relaxed test content.

assertion_results.text: The test change classification analysis is based on comparing base-branch and PR-branch file content (function additions, removals, assertion changes) — the structural and semantic assessment references test file content, not acceptance criteria or task requirements (constraint 1.18)
assertion_results.evidence: The structural summary in report.md references specific test file content: function counts (+1/-1 in purl_recommend.rs, +3 in purl_simplify.rs), assertion counts (+2/-1), and exact assertion text changes (the old assert_eq with qualifiers vs new assert_eq without). The semantic assessment references 'test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers function tested qualifier-specific behavior' and 'assertion change in test_recommend_purls_basic reflects the new expected response format' — these reference test file content and behavior, not acceptance criteria. While the semantic assessment does mention 'aligned with the task goal', the structural analysis itself is grounded in file content comparisons (function additions/removals, assertion changes between base and PR branches).

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: The report.md contains '#### Eval Quality -- N/A' (line 159) with details 'No eval result reviews found on this PR.' The summary table Test Quality row reads 'PASS | All test functions have doc comments; no repetitive test patterns detected; Eval Quality: N/A' — Eval Quality is listed as N/A and does not affect the Test Quality result.
